author: Clement of Rome
book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.

classification.cautionReason: Valued early Christian text, but not part of the biblical canon for mainstream Christian denominations.

lineage: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
These Exhortations are Confirmed by the Christian Faith, Which Proclaims the Misery of Sinful Conduct.

ccel.title: ANF09. The Gospel of Peter, The Diatessaron of Tatian, The Apocalypse of Peter, the Vision of Paul, The Apocalypse of the Virgin and Sedrach, The Testament of Abraham, The Acts of Xanthippe and Polyxena, The Narrative of Zosimus, The Apology of Aristides, The Epistles of Clement (complete text), Origen’s Commentary on John, Books 1–10, and Commentary on Matthew, Books 1, 2, and 10–14.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: Now the faith which is in Christ confirms all these [admonitions].

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: For He Himself by the Holy Ghost thus addresses us: “Come, ye children, hearken unto me; I will teach you the fear of the Lord.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: What man is he that desireth life, and loveth to see good days?

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: Keep thy tongue from evil, and thy lips from speaking guile.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: Depart from evil, and do good; seek peace, and pursue it.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: The eyes of the Lord are upon the righteous, and His ears are [open] unto their prayers.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: The face of the Lord is against them that do evil, to cut off the remembrance of them from the earth.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: The righteous cried, and the Lord heard him, and delivered him out of all his troubles.”

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: “Many are the stripes [appointed for] the wicked; but mercy shall compass those about who hope in the Lord.”
